title: City of Chaos - Let's Play - Part 2
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DPz-ktdaHII

Daniel (@dungeondive) revises his house rules for City of Chaos, slowing things down: roll a d8 and d6 and pick a movement value, end the turn on encountering any location, and draw an action card only when ending on an empty street tile — restoring the random encounters the first session blew past.

Bing joins the Pyrotechnics guild, gaining a rocket, flash powder and a smokescreen — and since Magi and Pyrotechnics are sworn enemies, he and Dizzy may now be at odds. Dizzy visits the mortuary and examines three corpses, lifting the undecayed 40-year-old body of the duelist Sif — a young man who died of grief after the head of the Pyrotechnics killed the woman he loved — and pocketing the mysterious, "no apparent use" Ring of Hisvet Sif. Bing finds the Duelists' Academy and a pawn shop, while a benign archbishop inexplicably rolls a curse onto Dizzy, docking his attacks.

The threads are tightening: a 40-year-old murder, a feud between guilds, and an heirloom ring whose purpose remains tantalisingly hidden. The city continues its sprawl.

----

A ring with "no apparent use" is practically a neon sign in a game like this — do you enjoy chasing those deferred mysteries, or find them frustrating?
